name: mcp server for unix pty control
description: The native MCP server for Unix PTY control gives AI models authentic, low-latency terminal access by spawning true pseudo-terminalsnot brittle AppleScript hacksso you can open persistent Bash, Python, or SSH sessions, run commands like “ssh prod.server,” and stream only fresh output thanks to smart buffering. Built on Tokio for fully asynchronous, non-blocking I/O, the service supports multi-session workflows, letting developers switch between concurrent prod-debug shells without interruption while JSON-RPC endpoints such as tools/call create_session handle lifecycle automation. This combination of real PTYs, session persistence, and high-performance async design makes the MCP server an ideal foundation for DevOps automation, cloud debugging, and AI-driven terminal orchestration.

This package vendors a subset of browser-use with minimal dependencies exposed as an mcp-server.

Note this does not wrap the full browser-use agent. There are other MCP's that do that. This one only exposes the internal tools for folks who want to roll their own agent loop.

Supports the following tools:

done
search_google
go_to_url
go_back
wait
click_element
input_text
switch_tab
open_tab
extract_content
scroll_down
scroll_up
send_keys
scroll_to_text
get_dropdown_options
select_dropdown_option
author: phact

name: MeterSphere MCP Server (Model Context Protocol)
description: MeterSphere MCP Server (Model Context Protocol Server) establishes a connection with the MCP Client through an HTTP SSE, STDIO, or Docker channel to expose testing-related tools and resources to LLMs. As an intelligent bridge between the test platform and the development environment, it completely breaks down the barriers between testing and R&D, allowing developers to acquire, execute, and analyse test resources directly in the IDE, code editor, or AI assistant without switching tools. Developers can use natural language instructions to allow large models to call the underlying testing capabilities, realise full-process operations such as interface testing, document acquisition, mock service creation, and compatibility verification, and move tests to the development stage, significantly shortening the feedback loop and improving R&D efficiency. At the same time, the test assets (use case library, environment configuration, assertion rules, etc.) configured by the test team can be seamlessly called by developers, ensuring the unification of testing standards and promoting the integrated practice of DevTestOps. Clients can discover and call tools to perform functional tests, interface tests, interface document acquisition, and other scenarios, realising the intelligence, automation, and collaboration of the testing process.
author: metersphere
